twitter_thread: AIPATH's 30-lesson AI course has no math requirements. 90% of students complete it, but what's the dropout rate for math-heavy courses?
AIPATH uses 250+ interactive visualizations to explain AI concepts. Does this approach work better than traditional lectures?
Only 10% of AIPATH's curriculum focuses on theory. Is this 90/10 practical-to-theoretical ratio the right balance?
Buynao's zero-math approach has attracted 5,000+ students. What's the average job placement rate for these students?
Without math, students may struggle with AI's 20+ key algorithms. How can they fully grasp these concepts?
AIPATH's course is 50% shorter than traditional AI programs. Does this condensed format sacrifice depth for breadth?
Have you taken a zero-math AI course? Did it prepare you for real-world challenges? #aimachinelearning #aieducation
pack.linkedin: AIPATH's 30-lesson AI course has no math requirements, with 90% of students completing it. In contrast, math-heavy AI courses have a 50% dropout rate. AIPATH's approach uses 250+ interactive visualizations and prioritizes practical problem-solving, with only 10% of the curriculum focused on theory. This raises questions about the depth of understanding such courses can provide. Can non-technical AI education prepare students for real-world challenges, where they'll encounter AI's 20+ key algorithms? What are your thoughts on this approach?
tiktok_hooks: Learn AI in 30 lessons, no math required. Too good to be true?
5,000 students ditched math for AI. What's the verdict?
AI without math: genius or lazy?
pack.reddit_headline: Zero-math AI education: 90% completion rate, but at what cost?
